moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to cure concentration and gross leverage flags. Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure. Increase QQQI to 25% for income and stability. Buy XLK for quality tech exposure. Hold a 15% cash buffer to preserve capital.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% monthly return with a focus on capital preservation. It significantly reduces risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and maintaining a substantial cash buffer, leading to lower potential drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to cure concentration and gross leverage flags. Sell SOXL to exit a weak, leveraged position. Trim SPAL to a small 5% exposure. Increase QQQI to 22% for balanced income. Buy XLK for quality tech exposure. Maintain a 10% cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for approximately 2.5% monthly growth with a risk-first approach. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad market exposure with quality tech, offering a moderate risk-reward profile.

moves.note: Press what is working, strong setup

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to cure concentration and gross leverage flags. Sell SOXL to reallocate capital from a weak position. Trim QQQI to 10% to shift towards growth. Add to SMH, and buy XLK and META, leveraging high-scoring setups. Maintain a 5% cash buffer.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% monthly growth by leaning into leverage and high-scoring setups. This approach significantly increases both potential upside and downside, with leveraged ETFs subject to daily decay and rebalancing, meaning substantial losses can occur quickly without strict stop discipline.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to cure concentration and gross leverage flags. Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETFs. Sell CRWD and SMH to reduce high-beta single name exposure. Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize income distributions. Maintain a 15% cash buffer for stability.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% monthly return by prioritizing steady distributions and low drawdown. While aiming for cash-like income, distributions are variable and the NAV can still decline in a selloff, meaning it is not a savings account and capital is still at risk.
